title: To permit the use of artificial intelligence in arbitration, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy aims to allow the use of artificial intelligence in arbitration, potentially enabling more efficient and streamlined dispute resolution processes. The specific provisions and requirements for AI use in arbitration may be outlined in the full document, which is not available.

title: To increase access to artificial intelligence through programs of the Department of Agriculture, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: This policy aims to make artificial intelligence more accessible through Department of Agriculture programs. It is intended to serve various purposes in addition to increasing AI access.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy document that aims to regulate and guide the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) models. However, without further information, it is unclear what specific regulations or guidelines the Act would establish.

title: A bill to amend the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to enable schools to teach elementary school and secondary school students about the dangers, limitations, and responsible use of artificial intelligence technologies, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill proposes to amend the 1965 Elementary and Secondary Education Act to allow schools to teach students about the dangers, limitations, and responsible use of artificial intelligence. The amendment aims to educate elementary and secondary school students on the safe use of artificial intelligence technologies.

title: Liquid Cooling for AI Act of 2025
action: Committee on Energy and Natural Resources Subcommittee on Energy. Hearings held. With printed Hearing: S.Hrg. 119-366.
summary: The Liquid Cooling for AI Act of 2025 is a policy document that likely aims to address the thermal management needs of artificial intelligence systems, potentially through the use of liquid cooling techniques. It may aim to provide guidelines or regulations to develop and implement more effective cooling methods for AI-related hardware and infrastructure.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Border Security and Enforcement.
summary: This AI policy document aims to test the use of artificial intelligence with anomaly detection at Arizona's land border crossings. The program's goal is to assess the effectiveness of this technology for U.S. Customs and Border Protection.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act aims to put a temporary stop to the construction of new artificial intelligence data centers in the area.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This law is called the Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act, but without more information, we can't provide a detailed summary.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This policy, the Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act, is intended to assess the risks that generative artificial intelligence may pose in relation to terrorism, but with limited information available. The exact details of the policy are unknown as no description is provided.

title: To require artificial intelligence chatbot providers to provide data privacy and security, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This bill aims to require artificial intelligence chatbot providers to protect users' data privacy and security. It serves other unspecified purposes related to artificial intelligence.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This is a policy document outlining regulations and laws related to the environmental impacts caused by artificial intelligence systems. The document is likely aimed at addressing and mitigating the negative effects of AI on the environment.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: The Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act aims to reduce the impact of AI data centers on household energy costs and protect families from financial strain. This act likely involves regulations or incentives to lower the energy consumption of AI data centers.

title: Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: The Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026 is a legislative proposal focused on addressing the intersection of aging populations and artificial intelligence. The specifics of the policy are not yet available to summarize.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to create an environment that fosters artificial intelligence innovation in the financial services sector. It likely seeks to balance the benefits and risks of AI adoption in finance.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote and support the development and use of artificial intelligence in the financial services industry. This act likely seeks to create policies and regulations that facilitate innovation while ensuring the security and integrity of financial transactions.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: This document aims to outline a policy called the 'Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act', which likely targets measures to safeguard consumers from misleading AI-related practices. The full details of the policy are not provided due to the lack of description.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy document aims to update the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to include a prize competition program focused on artificial intelligence research. The main objective is to promote advancements in AI by encouraging innovation and competition.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a legislation aimed at managing risks associated with the development and use of artificial intelligence. It likely contains guidelines and regulations to ensure AI systems are developed and used responsibly and safely.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This policy document, the Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act, is aimed at evaluating and managing the potential risks of terrorism associated with the development and deployment of generative artificial intelligence systems. The Act likely seeks to assess the threats posed by these systems to national security and public safety.
